meta.title: Islamic AI for your product
meta.description: Embed the same knowledge-backed Islamic AI behind Tanyalah Ustaz into your platform, app, or website.

hero.title: Bring Tanyalah Ustaz Islamic AI to your users
hero.subtitle: The same trusted Islamic AI behind {consumerApp}, now available for your platform, app, or website. One integration, cited answers, and no AI infrastructure to manage.

trust.body: Your integration uses the same curated knowledge base and answer quality as our app, built for teams who want to offer trusted Islamic guidance inside their own products.

features.title: Everything you need to integrate
features.subtitle: A complete pipeline behind a single API, so your team ships faster, without building AI infrastructure from scratch.

items.title: Grounded in the same knowledge base
items.body: Answers draw from the curated library that powers Tanyalah Ustaz: articles on fiqh, ibadah, aqidah, and akhlak. Scholarship-backed responses, not generic guesses.

items.title: Cited sources on every reply
items.body: Responses come with references to the source articles used. Build trust with your users by showing exactly where each answer comes from.

items.title: One simple integration
items.body: Send a user's question and get a reply back. No prompt engineering, no databases to run, no model providers to manage. We handle it all.

items.title: Conversation memory
items.body: Follow-up questions stay in context across multiple turns, just like a real conversation with a knowledgeable guide.

items.title: Category filtering
items.body: Scope answers to fiqh, ibadah, aqidah, akhlak, or general so responses match the context of the page your users are on.

items.title: Developer dashboard
items.body: API keys, live testing, chat logs, and usage insights: everything your team needs to launch and monitor your integration.

howItWorks.title: From question to cited answer in one step
howItWorks.subtitle: You send a message. We retrieve, ground, and generate the answer. Your users get guidance they can trust.

steps.title: Create your API key
steps.body: Sign up and generate an API key from the dashboard in seconds.

steps.title: Send a question
steps.body: Pass the user's message through our chat API. Optionally set a topic category for more relevant answers.

steps.title: We retrieve & ground
steps.body: We search the knowledge base for the most relevant passages and build a grounded response.

steps.title: Return a cited answer
steps.body: Your users get a clear, knowledge-backed reply, complete with the source articles behind it.

useCases.title: Built for products that serve Muslims
useCases.subtitle: Wherever your users have questions about their faith, give them grounded answers in context.

items.title: Islamic education platforms
items.body: Give students an always-available assistant that answers questions with references to real material.

items.title: Mosque & community apps
items.body: Add a Q&A helper for prayer, fasting, and daily practice, grounded in vetted content.

items.title: Fintech & halal commerce
items.body: Answer shariah-compliance questions inline, with sources your compliance team can verify.

items.title: Content & media sites
items.body: Turn your articles into an interactive assistant that keeps readers engaged and informed.

pricing.title: Start free, scale when you're ready
pricing.subtitle: Create an account and get an API key today. Talk to us when you need higher volume or a custom knowledge base.

developer.title: Starter
developer.subtitle: For building, testing, and early integrations.

partner.title: Partner
partner.subtitle: For production apps and growing teams.

faq.title: Questions, answered
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know before you integrate.

items.q: How is this different from tanyalahustaz.com?
items.a: This is the developer platform for businesses. {consumerApp} ({consumerUrl}) is where individuals ask questions directly. This API lets you embed the same knowledge-backed AI into your own product.

items.q: Is this login the same as tanyalahustaz.com?
items.a: No. Sign in here to manage your developer dashboard: API keys, testing, and usage. The {consumerApp} app has its own login for individuals. The two accounts are not interchangeable.

items.q: Where do the answers come from?
items.a: Answers are generated from the same curated Tanyalah Ustaz knowledge base used in the app. We find the most relevant passages for each question and use them to produce grounded, cited responses.

items.q: Do I need to run my own AI infrastructure?
items.a: No. We handle search, grounding, and generation. You make a single authenticated API call and receive a finished reply with sources.
Each request uses an API key you create in the dashboard. Keys can be revoked at any time. See the documentation for full authentication details.
Yes. The dashboard includes a live testing area that uses the same pipeline as production, so you can try questions and categories before writing any code.

items.q: Are the answers a substitute for a scholar?
items.a: No. Responses are AI-generated for guidance and convenience. For rulings on sensitive or personal matters, users should consult a qualified scholar. We recommend surfacing this clearly in your product.

knowledge.deleteConfirm: Delete this article and its embeddings? This cannot be undone.

overview.title: Developer platform
overview.lead: Integrate the same knowledge-backed Islamic AI that powers {consumerApp} into your product. Send a user question; we retrieve relevant knowledge, build a grounded prompt, and return an AI answer with cited sources.
howItWorksSteps: Your app sends a user question to POST /api/v1/chat.
We embed the question and search the knowledge base with semantic retrieval (RAG).
Relevant passages are injected into the AI prompt as context.
Earlier messages in the same session_id are included for follow-up questions.
We return the answer with source article references.
quickStartSteps: Create a developer account and sign in.
Go to Dashboard → API Keys and create a key.
POST a message to /api/v1/chat from your backend, or use the playground to test first.
overview.openapiDescription: Import the machine-readable API spec into Postman, Swagger UI, or your SDK generator.

authentication.title: Authentication
authentication.lead: Protected API endpoints require an API key. Keys are created in the {dashboardLink} and stored as SHA-256 hashes. We never store the full key.
authentication.loginVsAppBody: {signInLink} on this site accesses the {dashboardName} (API keys, live testing, usage). It is separate from the {consumerAppLink}, where individuals ask questions. Accounts are not shared between the two products.
authentication.apiKeyHeadersDescription: Send your key on every API request using either header:
authentication.keyFormatDescription: Keys are prefixed with tlh_live_ followed by a random secret. Revoked keys return 401 Unauthorized.
authentication.dashboardVsApiDescription: The dashboard uses email/password login. Your API integration uses API keys. Signing into the dashboard does not authenticate API requests, and API keys do not grant dashboard access.

endpoints.description: Returns the authenticated account profile and API key metadata.
Main integration endpoint. Sends a user message, retrieves relevant knowledge from the Tanyalah Ustaz library, and returns an AI reply with sources.
List conversation sessions for your account, newest activity first. Supports ?limit and ?offset.
Delete all chat history for your account. Requires ?confirm=all.
Get chronological chat history for a session. Pagination limit/offset apply to conversation turns (each turn is one user + one assistant message).
Delete one chat session and all of its turns.
Usage stats for the current API key over the last 30 days.
OpenAPI 3.1 specification for this API. No authentication required.

errors.title: Errors & rate limits
errors.description: All API errors return a consistent JSON shape with a stable code, human-readable message, and request_id for support.
errors.formatDescription: Failed requests return HTTP 4xx/5xx with this body:
errors.requestIdDescription: Every response includes an X-Request-Id header. You may pass your own X-Request-Id (8–64 alphanumeric characters) and we will echo it back. Include request_id when contacting support.
errors.rateLimitDescription: Authenticated endpoints return rate-limit headers on success and 429 responses when limits are exceeded.
errors.rateLimitMinute: Remaining requests in the current minute
errors.rateLimitDay: Remaining requests for the current UTC day

openapi.title: OpenAPI
openapi.description: Machine-readable API specification for tooling, SDK generation, and contract testing.
openapi.specDescription: Download or import this URL into your API client:
openapi.toolsPostman: Postman: Import → Link → paste the specification URL
openapi.toolsCodegen: openapi-generator or Speakeasy: generate client SDKs from the spec
openapi.toolsSwagger: Swagger UI: load the spec URL for interactive exploration

homeDemo.sampleQuestion: Can a traveler combine Dhuhr and Asr?
homeDemo.sampleResponse: Yes. A traveler may combine Dhuhr and Asr (jama') during a journey, either advancing Asr into Dhuhr's time or delaying Dhuhr into Asr's time…
